title: trichera arvensis subsp. rosea
intro: Trichera arvensis subsp. rosea, also known as the rose-flowered corn marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Trichera arvensis subsp. rosea include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
